what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry that summarizes and characterizes a living requirements specification for the ConceptV design system, recording its structure, coded requirements, confidence statuses, conventions, standout ideas, and intended contribution to a fused codebase.
observations: The file describes a requirements document organized into lettered sections A through G covering fidelity, structure, parametric axes, reuse/generation, motion, interactivity, and output surfaces.
Each requirement is tagged with a confidence status using a green/yellow/blue legend tied to how many ingest folders confirm it.
A NORTH STAR states that requirements must be expressed as rules, ratios, tokens, and parameters so design is computed from content and axis position rather than hand-authored.
Section A fixes visual fidelity details such as a near-white tonal ladder, a gold-bronze-tan color ramp, zoning-as-depth, textures, and explicit restraint guardrails listing what to refuse.
Section C defines independent composable axes: Surface, Level-of-Detail, Register/pace, Density, Theme, and the computation f(content, axis).
Section D covers typed template slots, client-variant as a parameter, invariant skeleton rules, and a catalog of ten diagram types.
The file notes an append-only log at the bottom tracking which folders established or confirmed which requirements.
The header cross-references sibling documents SYNTHESIS-PLAN.md, AXES.md, SYSTEM-GAPS.md, and DIAGRAMS.md.
No code symbols are present; the content is entirely descriptive metadata.
standouts: The restraint guardrails define the system by what it refuses, not only by what it permits.
Zoning marks containment depth rather than semantic category, inverting common design practice.
Invariance is treated as a node-type property: numbers and diagrams never prune, while prose is LOD-variable.
conventions: Coded requirement IDs such as A1-A7, B1-B5, C1-C6 for stable cross-reference.
Status-emoji confidence ladder tied to evidence count across ingest folders.
Append-only growth: new ingest folders append or upgrade requirements.
Pairs with sibling docs by reference rather than restating their content.
Follows a JSON interpretive-ledger schema with neutral, file-local commentary.
concerns: Confidence statuses are asserted without listing the specific folders or evidence that produced each grade in this file.
References to vt-* and vi ingest samples are mentioned as evidence but not explained or enumerated here.
The distinction between Register/pace and the LOD/Density axes is described but remains abstract without concrete examples in this file.
notes: A neutral interpretive snapshot of an evidence-graded design-system requirements contract, emphasizing computable rules and refusal guardrails.
questions: What concrete behavior distinguishes Register/pace from LOD and Density when it is said to drive them together yet remain separable?
What are the specific vt-* and vi ingest samples cited as evidence for several requirements?
How is the confidence count verified, and which folders contributed to each status?
Which exact output surfaces are enumerated in Section G?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file presents itself as an interpretive summary of what the requirements document does and achieves, and the body is consistent with that purpose.
apparent_themes: Design as computation over an invariant core
Parameters over variants, ratios over pixels, rules over one-off layouts
Definition by refusal through restraint guardrails
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to turn a design-system specification into a machine-readable, evidence-backed contract so that new, never-seen components can be generated while still obeying the system's DNA.
novelty: Treating each requirement as a computable rule and grading every requirement by cross-folder evidence count, while also defining the system through explicit refusals.
contribution: Contributes a structured, confidence-graded summary of the ConceptV requirements including visual fidelity rules, parametric axes, template and generation rules, motion and interactivity needs, output surfaces, and refusal guardrails, packaged as neutral interpretive metadata for later fusion.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger entry summarizing the ConceptV design-system requirements spec as a set of computable, evidence-graded rules with parametric axes, template logic, motion/interactivity needs, and explicit refusal guardrails.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the requirements document's intent as a generative, machine-interpretable contract that can produce on-brand output across surfaces and detail levels while tracking confidence from real samples.
